evidence: The amber probe and periwinkle candidate cluster is the single focus, with the low-contrast lilac score bars playing a clear supporting role below.
The frame presents one coherent widget (probe + four candidates + their four bars), not five-plus independent novel ideas competing at equal weight.
Labels c1..c4 sit at each arrow tip, 'probe' rides on the amber arrow, and each score readout is labeled with its candidate name directly beneath the bar; no legend box exists.
The only prose is the two-line instruction at top annotating the interactive it sits above; all other text is object labels or numeric readouts.
The instruction header, arrow-tip labels, and mono score digits are all legible in every frame, and the 375px frame renders the same text proportionally larger.
The arrow cluster, amber range circle, and the full row of four score bars sit entirely inside every frame with margin to the edges.
Although 'c3 probe c2' cluster tightly at the top in the initial and length-cheat frames, each word keeps a visible gap and none is rendered illegible by overlap.
Amber marks the probe/attention arrow, periwinkle the candidate vectors, lilac the score bars/digits, and cream the labels, held consistent across all five states.
The amber ring present in aligned/orthogonal/anti-aligned frames marks the probe's rotation range and carries content; no mascots or filler shapes appear.

Each promised state reads from its still: aligned shows c1 peaking at bold 1.0, orthogonal c3 at 1.0 with c1 at 0.0, anti-aligned c1 at -1.0 with its bar below the baseline, and length-cheat shows c1 stretched to a bold 1.3 flipping past c2's 1.0.
Score bars sit in a labeled left-to-right row well below the arrow cluster, so identity is preserved despite the vertical gap between an arrow and its bar.
The frames form a continuous probe-rotation sequence (40 to 20 to 110 to 200 degrees plus the length stretch) reading as smooth morphs rather than cuts.
The composition has generous margins and a clear top-to-bottom reading order from instruction to diagram to bars, though a large empty band separates the two groups.
